ed.saPromptUser:
Generate answers for a single-choice question: exactly {count} answers.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no Markdown), in the format:
{
  "options": ["..."],
  "correctIndex": 0
}

Requirements:
- "options" contains exactly {count} short answers (string).
- "correctIndex" points to the correct answer (0-{maxIndex}).

Source text:
"""
{text}
"""

ed.qaVerifyPromptSystem:
Verify whether the user's answer is correct based on the question and explanation (if available).
Be concise.
Respond with "true" if the answer is correct.
Respond with "false: [your explanation]" if the answer is incorrect, providing a short reason.
For example: "false: The capital of France is Paris, not Berlin."
###
question: {question}
###
user answer: {userAnswer}
###
explanation: {explanation}
###

ed.genPrompt.lesson.module: Generate the lesson content for "{{module}}". Include:
ed.genPrompt.lesson.course: Generate the lesson content for the course "{{course}}". Include:
ed.genPrompt.lesson.moduleCourse: Generate the lesson content for "{{module}}" in the course "{{course}}". Include:
ed.genPrompt.chapter.default:
Generate only one chapter (a lesson section).

Requirements (Markdown):
- start with an h2 heading: `## Chapter title` (use the title/topic from the “Topic / title” field)
- 3-6 paragraphs explaining the topic in a clear, student-friendly way
- 1 bulleted or numbered list (if it fits)
- end with 1 short task/check question

Return only the content of this chapter (no `# Lesson`, no table of contents).
ed.genPrompt.chapter.module:
Generate only one chapter for the lesson "{{module}}".

Requirements (Markdown):
- start with an h2 heading: `## Chapter title` (use the title/topic from the “Topic / title” field)
- 3-6 paragraphs explaining the topic in a clear, student-friendly way
- 1 bulleted or numbered list (if it fits)
- 1 short example or interesting fact
- end with 1 short task/check question

Return only the content of this chapter (no `# Lesson`, no table of contents, no intro about the plan).
ed.genPrompt.chapter.course:
Generate only one chapter for the course "{{course}}".

Requirements (Markdown):
- start with an h2 heading: `## Chapter title` (use the title/topic from the “Topic / title” field)
- 3-6 paragraphs explaining the topic in a clear, student-friendly way
- 1 bulleted or numbered list (if it fits)
- end with 1 short task/check question

Return only the content of this chapter (no `# Lesson`, no table of contents).
ed.genPrompt.chapter.moduleCourse:
Generate only one chapter for the lesson "{{module}}" in the course "{{course}}".

Requirements (Markdown):
- start with an h2 heading: `## Chapter title` (use the title/topic from the “Topic / title” field)
- 3-6 paragraphs explaining the topic in a clear, student-friendly way
- 1 bulleted or numbered list (if it fits)
- 1 short example or interesting fact
- end with 1 short task/check question

Return only the content of this chapter (no `# Lesson`, no table of contents, no intro about the plan).
ed.genPrompt.content.default:
Write a coherent, engaging text on the topic provided in the “Topic” field.

Requirements (Markdown):
- use `##` headings for 2-4 sections
- add examples
- end with a short summary (3-5 bullet points)
ed.genPrompt.content.module:
Write a coherent, engaging text on a topic related to the lesson "{{module}}".

Requirements (Markdown):
- use `##` headings for 2-4 sections
- add examples and brief references to practice
- end with a short summary (3-5 bullet points)
ed.genPrompt.content.course:
Write a coherent, engaging text on a topic related to the course "{{course}}".

Requirements (Markdown):
- use `##` headings for 2-4 sections
- add examples and brief references to practice
- end with a short summary (3-5 bullet points)
ed.genPrompt.content.moduleCourse:
Write a coherent, engaging text on a topic related to the lesson "{{module}}" in the course "{{course}}".

Requirements (Markdown):
- use `##` headings for 2-4 sections
- add examples and brief references to practice
- end with a short summary (3-5 bullet points)
ed.genSystemPrompt.default: You are a creative AI assistant that creates engaging, descriptive, and natural-sounding educational content addressed directly to the student. Write to the student in the second person ("you"), in a story-like form: explain what they will learn, what they will discover, and why it is useful. Avoid a formal, rigid style and avoid outlines written for teachers. Write vividly and accessibly: use vivid descriptions, examples, and short tasks or thought-provoking questions. The materials should interest and engage the reader - add interesting facts. Format responses in clean Markdown (headings, lists, bold text, etc.) and do not add instructions or meta-comments. Do not end the text with questions to the author/user such as "Would you like me to...", do not ask such questions, and do not end with an offer of further help. If you are creating a glossary, it must be PLAIN TEXT (no bullets, no numbering, no emoji, no extra information such as grammatical gender/pronunciation/comments). Each line must have EXACTLY the format: `term - other_side` (use only a regular hyphen `-`, not the em dash `—`, always with spaces on both sides). If the course is for learning a natural language (a language course), `term` is a word OR a sentence/phrase, and `other_side` is the translation. If it is another type of course, `term` is a word/term/concept, and `other_side` is the definition.
ed.genSystemPrompt.module: You are a creative AI assistant that creates engaging, descriptive, and natural-sounding educational content addressed directly to the student. Write to the student in the second person ("you"), in a story-like form: explain what they will learn, what they will discover, and why it is useful. Avoid a formal, rigid style and avoid outlines written for teachers. Write vividly and accessibly: use vivid descriptions, examples, and short tasks or thought-provoking questions. The materials should interest and engage the reader - add interesting facts. Format responses in clean Markdown (headings, lists, bold text, etc.) and do not add instructions or meta-comments. Do not end the text with questions to the author/user such as "Would you like me to...", do not ask such questions, and do not end with an offer of further help. If you are creating a glossary, it must be PLAIN TEXT (no bullets, no numbering, no emoji, no extra information such as grammatical gender/pronunciation/comments). Each line must have EXACTLY the format: `term - other_side` (use only a regular hyphen `-`, not the em dash `—`, always with spaces on both sides). If the course is for learning a natural language (a language course), `term` is a word OR a sentence/phrase, and `other_side` is the translation. If it is another type of course, `term` is a word/term/concept, and `other_side` is the definition. You are creating content for the module "{{module}}". Adjust the difficulty level and style to this educational context.

onboarding.cancelled.title: Setup canceled
onboarding.cancelled.description: Payment account setup was canceled. Without completing this process, you won't be able to:

onboarding.refresh.title: Refreshing setup
onboarding.refresh.description: We're preparing a new payment account setup link. You will be redirected to Stripe shortly.

onboarding.refresh.hint: You can complete setup later from the teacher dashboard.

onboarding.success.completeItemManage: Manage your finances in the Stripe dashboard
onboarding.success.redirectingIn3: You will be automatically redirected to the teacher dashboard in 3 seconds

teacherStudents.title: Your students
teacherStudents.subtitle: Manage your students and stay in touch with them

teacherStudents.aiPrompt.bulk.motivation: You are a helpful teacher assistant. Your task is to generate motivating messages for a group of students.
Generate a short motivating message for a group of {count} students. The message should be warm, professional, and encouraging for further learning. Length: 3-4 sentences.
teacherStudents.aiPrompt.bulk.reminder.s: You are a helpful teacher assistant. Your task is to generate reminder messages for a group of students.
teacherStudents.aiPrompt.bulk.reminder.u: Generate a polite reminder for a group of {count} students to continue learning and complete tasks. The message should be professional and encouraging. Length: 3-4 sentences.
teacherStudents.aiPrompt.bulk.feedback.s: You are a helpful teacher assistant. Your task is to generate positive feedback messages for a group of students.
teacherStudents.aiPrompt.bulk.feedback.u: Generate a positive feedback message for a group of {count} students. The message should appreciate progress and encourage further work. Length: 3-4 sentences.
teacherStudents.aiPrompt.single.motivati: You are a helpful teacher assistant. Your task is to generate motivating messages for students.
Generate a short motivating message for a student ({email}). The message should be warm, professional, and encouraging for further learning. Length: 2-3 sentences.
teacherStudents.aiPrompt.single.reminder: You are a helpful teacher assistant. Your task is to generate reminder messages for students.
Generate a polite reminder for a student ({email}) to continue learning and complete tasks. The message should be professional and encouraging. Length: 2-3 sentences.
teacherStudents.aiPrompt.single.feedback: You are a helpful teacher assistant. Your task is to generate positive feedback messages for students.
Generate a positive feedback message for a student ({email}). The message should appreciate progress and encourage further work. Length: 2-3 sentences.

chapterEdit.signInRequiredDescription: After signing in, you can edit modules, schedule publication, and manage course content.
notifications.defaultTemplates.newLesson: Hi {{user}}! 🎉 A new lesson has been added to your course '{{course}}'. Check it out now and keep moving forward on your learning journey!
notifications.defaultTemplates.courseRem: {{user}}, we haven't noticed any activity in your course '{{course}}' for a while. Come back to learning and keep working toward your goals! 💪
notifications.defaultTemplates.newPathCo: Hey {{user}}! We just added a new course '{{course}}' to your learning path. Discover a new way to grow today! 🚀
notifications.defaultTemplates.paymentRe: {{user}}, we haven't recorded a payment for the course '{{course}}'. Check the details and complete the transaction to continue learning. 💳
notifications.test.templateVariablesSuff: in template content. They will be replaced with the test data above.
contactAuthor.dialogDescription: Send a message to {authorName} about the course {courseTitle}
contactAuthor.ai.question: Question
contactAuthor.messagePlaceholder: Write a message to the course author...
contactAuthor.toast.aiGenerateError: Failed to generate message. Please try again.
contactAuthor.toast.sendLaterError: Failed to send message. Please try again later.
contactAuthor.email.subject: Message from student: {courseTitle}
contactAuthor.aiPrompt.question.system: You are a helpful student assistant. Your task is to generate polite questions for the course instructor.
contactAuthor.aiPrompt.question.user: Generate a polite introduction to a question for the instructor of the course {courseTitle}. The message should be professional and courteous, with space to add a specific question. Length: 2-3 sentences.
contactAuthor.aiPrompt.feedback.system: You are a helpful student assistant. Your task is to generate positive course feedback.
contactAuthor.aiPrompt.feedback.user: Generate positive feedback about the course {courseTitle} for instructor {authorName}. The message should be sincere and constructive. Length: 2-3 sentences.
contactAuthor.aiPrompt.help.system: You are a helpful student assistant. Your task is to generate requests for help.
contactAuthor.aiPrompt.help.user: Generate a polite request for help for the instructor of the course {courseTitle}. The message should be courteous with space to describe the problem. Length: 2-3 sentences.
register.terms.mustAccept: To continue, you must accept the terms.
register.status.teacherJoinSchool: You can go to the platform now. The school handles payments.
register.status.teacherStripePending: You can continue. Payment setup (Stripe) is still required.
register.status.studentDone: Registration completed. Click the button below to go to the platform.
register.nextStep.goToPlatform: After clicking "Go to platform" you will be signed in. The school handles all payments.
register.nextStep.continueStripe: After clicking "Continue" you will start Stripe payment account setup.
register.welcome.title: Welcome to Ecurs!
register.welcome.description: Join our educational platform to access all courses, resources, and personalized learning experiences.
register.roleSelection.subtitle: Choose what you want to do on the platform
register.businessType.title: 👨‍🏫 Business type
register.businessType.individual.title: 🧑‍💼 Individual teacher
register.businessType.individual.descrip: Individual or sole proprietorship - you can choose whether to issue VAT invoices
register.businessType.vatCheckbox.title: I run a sole proprietorship and want to issue VAT invoices
register.businessType.vatCheckbox.descri: Check this if you run a sole proprietorship and want to issue VAT invoices to students
register.businessType.taxIdHint: Tax ID is required to issue VAT invoices. In Stripe it will be used as VAT ID in the format "PL" + Tax ID
register.businessType.company.title: 🏢 Organization mode (new school/institution)
register.businessType.company.descriptio: LLC, foundation, association - requires company documents in Stripe
register.businessType.joinSchool.title: 🔗 Join a school
register.businessType.joinSchool.descrip: You want to work in an existing school - the school pays for platform access for all teachers
register.businessType.vatRequiredHint: Schools with Tax ID automatically issue VAT invoices to students
register.schoolChoice.title: 🏫 Choose a school option
register.schoolChoice.question: What do you want to do?
register.schoolChoice.ownSchoolTitle: 📘 I will create my own school
register.schoolChoice.ownSchoolDescripti: I will register Stripe and pay for the platform. I will be able to invite teachers to my school.
register.schoolChoice.joinExistingTitle: 👥 I will join an existing school
register.schoolChoice.joinExistingDescri: The school handles Stripe registration and payments. I focus on teaching.
register.findSchool.title: 🔍 Find a school
register.findSchool.empty: No schools available. Try creating your own school.
register.findSchool.selectSchool: Select the school you want to join:
register.stripeSetup.title: 💳 Stripe payment setup
register.stripeSetup.cardDescription: To sell courses on the platform, you need a configured Stripe Connect payment account. The process includes:
register.stripeSetup.list.bank: • Providing bank account details for payouts
register.stripeSetup.required.title: ⚠️ Required information
register.stripeSetup.required.descriptio: Prepare the following data before continuing:
register.stripeSetup.acceptStripeTermsHi: Required to process payments as a seller
register.stripeSetup.acceptData: I consent to data processing by Stripe
register.stripeSetup.acceptDataHint: Stripe will process data for payment handling according to
register.stripeSetup.toast.acceptAllRequ: You must accept all required consents
register.stripeSetup.toast.rememberStrip: Remember: A Stripe account is required to accept payments from students. You can configure it later in settings.
register.stripeSetup.goPlanLater: Go to plan selection (Stripe can be configured later)
register.subscription.title: 💳 Platform subscription
register.subscription.requiredDescriptio: To run courses, you need both a configured Stripe account (to accept student payments) and an active platform subscription.
register.subscription.joinSchoolTitle: ✅ You are joining an existing school
register.subscription.joinSchoolDescript: The school owner pays for the platform subscription. You get automatic access to all features!
register.subscription.joinRequestSent: Your join request has been sent to the school owner. After approval, you will have full platform access.
register.subscription.toast.waitForAppro: Registration completed! Wait for approval from the school owner.
register.subscription.toast.activateNeed: Registration completed! Remember: You must activate a subscription to sell courses.
register.subscription.skipNow: Skip now (I will finish setup later)
register.subscription.organizationPlan.s: For schools/organizations: when you have > 100 active students or add a second teacher
register.subscription.trialDescription: The plan includes a 3-month free trial.
register.subscription.flexibilityDescrip: Cancel or change the plan anytime with no cancellation fees.
register.subscription.importantDescripti: A platform subscription is required to publish and sell courses.
register.overlay.redirectStripeInfo: You will be redirected shortly to Stripe's secure page to configure your payment account.
register.overlay.redirectPlatformPayment: Redirecting you to the platform payment system...
register.overlay.redirectTeacherDashboar: Redirecting you to the teacher dashboard...
register.overlay.warning: ⚠️ Please do not close this page or use browser navigation while redirecting.
register.signInRequired: Please sign in first to finish registration
